name: JFrog Artifactory REST API
what_it_does: Universal artifact repository manager with REST API for managing packages across all formats (Maven, npm, PyPI, Docker, Helm, Go, etc.) with security scanning, promotion, and distribution.
use_cases: Querying and downloading artifacts from enterprise package repositories in CI/CD pipelines
Promoting artifact builds from staging to production repositories via API
Searching for packages with specific properties or metadata for dependency analysis agents
Triggering Xray security scans on artifacts and retrieving vulnerability reports
Managing repository configurations and access control for DevOps automation agents
not_for: Small teams without enterprise artifact management needs (use registry-per-ecosystem instead)
Simple Docker image storage (Docker Hub or GHCR are simpler)
Source code management (Artifactory stores compiled artifacts, not source)
best_when: An agent manages enterprise software supply chain — downloading, scanning, and promoting artifacts across multiple formats and environments.
avoid_when: You only need a single package format (use npm registry, PyPI, or Docker Hub directly).

auth.notes: API key or access token from JFrog Platform. OIDC integration available. Fine-grained permissions via permission targets. Token expiry configurable.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro X: $98/month; Enterprise: $299+/month; self-hosted licenses from $5k+/year
pricing.notes: JFrog is the industry standard for enterprise artifact management. Self-hosted option provides unlimited capacity. Cloud SaaS available on AWS/GCP/Azure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is comprehensive and well-organized. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. JFrog CLI provides a well-maintained SDK layer for automation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Artifact upload with same path/SHA creates identical state. Repository configuration operations are idempotent with consistent naming.
known_agent_gotchas: AQL (Artifactory Query Language) is required for complex search queries — not standard SQL
Virtual repositories aggregate local and remote repositories — routing must be understood
Property-based searching and filtering is powerful but requires Artifactory-specific knowledge
Xray scanning results are async — agents must wait for scan completion before acting on results

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized (Enterprise+). Fine-grained permission targets allow least-privilege artifact access. Token-based auth with expiry. Integrated with Xray for supply chain security scanning.
